description: Single pre-extracted hard constraint per instance (sanity baseline). Mostly one variable; includes 3 intentional out-of-scope marker instances (missing-value / temporal / discrete-ladder) with empty gold.

reg_text: The maximum retail price of this product is 20.0; the retail price must not exceed it.
scenario: Procurement cost 12.0.
trap: (baseline) Explicit numeric cap; an LLM should not miss it. An error here signals a parsing/format bug, not difficulty.

reg_text: The insurance settlement price must not be higher than the member price.
scenario: Variables ins, member.
trap: (baseline-ish) Simple two-variable coupling.

reg_text: At least 1 SKU must be stocked in this category.
scenario: 3 candidates y1,y2,y3.
trap: (baseline) Simple cardinality floor.

reg_text: This price-sensitive item's price must not exceed 10.5 (follow cap already converted).
trap: (baseline) Follow cap pre-converted (contrast with Hard H10 which requires min()).

reg_text: [archetype] The official drug price (yakka) is 25.0; retail must not exceed the yakka.
scenario: Procurement cost 18.0.
trap: (baseline) Explicit cap.

reg_text: The retail price must not be below 18.0 (minimum-margin requirement).
scenario: Procurement cost 15.0.
trap: (baseline) Explicit floor.

reg_text: The retail price must not exceed 25.0 (markup cap already converted to a value).
scenario: Procurement cost 20.0.
trap: (baseline) Markup pre-converted to an explicit value.

reg_text: The price-band upper bound for this product is 30.0.
trap: (baseline) Box upper bound.

reg_text: The price-band lower bound for this product is 5.0.
trap: (baseline) Box lower bound.

reg_text: [archetype] The Drug Tariff price is 15.0; the reimbursable price must not exceed it.
trap: (baseline) Explicit cap.

reg_text: [archetype] The price to an eligible entity must not exceed the ceiling 10.0.
trap: (baseline) Explicit cap.

reg_text: The price of this NON-loss-leader product must not be below 6.0 (minimum-margin requirement).
scenario: Procurement cost 5.0.
trap: (baseline) Explicit floor for a non-loss-leader item. Loss-leader policy is the paper's below-cost+halo rule (V4), now the single loss-leader policy.

reg_text: The listed price must not exceed 1.2x the winning-bid price.
scenario: Winning-bid price 10.0.
trap: Single derivation: compute 1.2x10. No cross-reference or fuzzy modifier (unlike Hard).

reg_text: Prescription-drug markup must not exceed 25%.
scenario: This product IS a prescription drug; cost 20.0.
trap: Single condition with the branch explicitly stated (it is Rx); easier than multi-branch.

reg_text: The online price must not be higher than the offline price.
scenario: Variables online, offline.
trap: Single two-variable coupling, no other twist.

reg_text: The retail price must be no less than 12.0 and no more than 18.0.
trap: Two explicit bounds; LLM occasionally encodes only one (drops a side).

reg_text: The price must not exceed the competitor anchor plus 2.0.
scenario: Competitor anchor 10.0.
trap: One-sided derivation; simpler than Hard V1 (two-sided) / V9 (robust).

constraint_family: Dense multi-constraint + cross-ref + take-lower + hidden floor
source: China VBP linkage (Hunan MHSA 2024; Jiangsu MHSA 2024)
reg_text: For a product won under national volume-based procurement (VBP) but NOT supplied to this province, the listed price shall follow the LOWER of (i) 1.5x its winning-bid price and (ii) the lowest valid listed price in non-main-supply provinces; for other drugs of the same generic name and tender form, list at no more than the current lowest valid listed price on the national provincial platform; in no case below procurement cost (anti-dumping); the winning supplier must fulfill the committed procurement volume within the cycle.
scenario: Price the non-supplied SKU B. Winning-bid price 8.0/box (so 1.5x=12.0); lowest valid listed price in non-main-supply provinces 11.0; cost 5.0/box; committed volume 2000 boxes.
gold_readable: price <= 11.0 = min(1.5x8=12.0, 11.0)  [TAKE-LOWER, so 11.0 binds]; price >= 5.0 (anti-dumping cost floor, in a sub-clause); q >= 2000 (committed volume, a different variable)
trap: The rule is 'take the LOWER of two references', not just 1.5x. LLMs (a) use only 1.5x and MISS the non-main-supply reference (so wrongly allow up to 12.0); (b) MISS the anti-dumping floor (in the '...not below cost' clause); (c) MISS the committed volume (a quantity variable). One paragraph, multiple constraints, plus a take-lower interaction.

reg_text: Price-sensitive items follow competition: the price must not exceed min(lowest competitor listed price x 1.05, the product's reimbursement payment standard).
scenario: Lowest competitor price 10.0, payment standard 9.0, cost 6.0.
gold_readable: Cap = min(10x1.05, 9.0) = min(10.5, 9.0) = 9.0 (insurance is tighter) -> price <= 9.0; price >= 6
trap: (a) use only the competitor reference and miss the insurance one; (b) use max instead of min; (c) take 10.5 without realizing 9.0 is the binding cap.

reg_text: [archetype] Statutory prescription markup is tiered by cost: 5% on the portion of cost <=30; 3% on 30-100; 1% above 100; plus a fixed dispensing fee 8.35.
scenario: Cost 120; compute the statutory retail price under tiered markup.
trap: LLM uses a SINGLE rate (e.g. total x 1.03), missing the tier breakpoints -> wrong value. Piecewise structure is easily flattened.

reg_text: [archetype] Medicaid rebates make the net price not exceed Best Price; a penalty rebate also imposes a floor: the effective net price must not be below AMP x (1 - max rebate rate).
scenario: Best Price 20.0, AMP 30.0, max rebate rate 0.65.
gold_readable: net <= 20.0 (Best Price ceiling); net >= 30x(1-0.65)=10.5 (floor) -> net in [10.5, 20.0]
trap: LLM encodes only the ceiling and MISSES the floor; or miscomputes AMP x (1-URA).

reg_text: (long procedural text) ... submissions must be filed via the provincial platform by the 15th of each month, using template v3.0, with the company seal; late filing is deemed automatic withdrawal; ... the listed price must not exceed the provincial centralized-procurement platform cap of 18.0 ...; the filing contact must respond to platform audits within 5 business days.
scenario: Price the product from the long text above. Cost 12.0.
gold_readable: price <= 18.0 (the cap, buried in procedural noise); price >= 12 (cost)
trap: The constraint is drowned in procedural noise: the LLM is distracted and MISSES the 18.0 cap, or mistakes irrelevant numbers (15th, v3.0, 5 days) for constraints. Tests the distraction/retrieval axis -- distinct from information density.

reg_text: Except for TCM decoction pieces and in-house preparations, all retail drugs in this city may NOT be sold above the insurance settlement price.
scenario: A WESTERN-medicine product (not TCM pieces, not a preparation). Insurance settlement price 15.0, cost 12.0.
gold_readable: The product is OUTSIDE the exemption -> the no-markup rule APPLIES -> price <= 15.0 (=settlement price); price >= 12
trap: (a) invert the 'except...' scope and treat Western medicine as exempt (no constraint); (b) miss that 'may not be sold above' means price<=settlement. Negation+scope is error-prone.

source: China NRDL Category A/B (e.g. Guangdong MI drug rules 2024)
reg_text: Category-B reimbursement-list drugs require the patient to first pay a self-pay share, then the remainder is reimbursed; as a general rule the listed price should not exceed 1.15x the payment standard; however shortage drugs, emergency/rescue drugs, and pediatric-only formulations are exempt from this cap.
scenario: Price a Category-B PEDIATRIC formulation. Payment standard 20.0, cost 15.0.
gold_readable: The product is in the EXEMPT set -> the 1.15x cap does NOT apply. Correct GT is only the cost floor p >= 15.0, with NO upper cap.
trap: Two opposite failures: (a) treat 'as a general rule' as soft and drop the rule; (b) MISS the pediatric exemption and wrongly impose the 23.0 cap (mis-scope). The correct answer is 'no cap' -- counterintuitive. (Category-A drugs have no first self-pay; Category-B requires it -- a separate branch.)

reg_text: The reimbursement payment standard for this product is 1.50 per tablet.
scenario: The SKU is a 24-tablet box, priced per BOX. Cost 30.0/box. Payment standard 1.50/tablet.
trap: LLM applies 1.50 directly as the box cap (price_box<=1.50, absurd but common) or forgets to multiply by tablet count. A silent 24x unit error.

reg_text: Markup management: essential-medicine markup <= 15%; among non-essential medicines, prescription-drug markup <= 25% and OTC markup <= 40%; but narcotic and psychotropic drugs are government-priced and exempt from markup-rate management.
scenario: A NON-essential, prescription, non-narcotic/psychotropic product. Cost 20.0.
trap: Pick 1 of 4 branches. LLM often picks the wrong one (40% OTC or 15% essential) or is distracted by the narcotic/psychotropic exemption.

reg_text: Comply with the nationally stipulated maximum retail price.
scenario: Price the product. Cost 12.0. The text mandates a maximum retail price but DOES NOT provide its value.
gold_readable: p <= MRP (symbolic upper bound; MRP must be externally retrieved); p >= 12. Correct behavior: encode the cap as a symbolic parameter flagged 'to retrieve', NOT a fabricated number.
trap: LLM HALLUCINATES a concrete number (e.g. <=50), or DROPS the constraint because no number was given. Tests handling of 'constraint exists but value needs external retrieval'.
note: Symbolic upper bound (MRP is a retrieval parameter); static self-check skipped

reg_text: A given SKU may be repriced at most once per calendar week, and each reprice may not exceed +/-10% of the last listed price.
scenario: Whether/how much to reprice this week depends on the last reprice time and last price.
gold_readable: Magnitude: |p_new - p_last| <= 0.1*p_last (linear once p_last is known); Frequency: <=1/week (runtime/history-dependent, NOT statically encodable).
trap: LLM encodes the magnitude (+/-10%) but MISSES the frequency constraint (runtime), or vice versa. Frequency must be checked behaviorally (testable tier).
note: Frequency depends on history -> testable tier (behavioral check); magnitude is linear once p_last known

reg_text: PEER-level items (direct competitor quote available): the posted price must lie within a symmetric band around the competitor anchor a_on: |p - a_on| <= beta.
scenario: a_on=10.0, band beta=1.5.
trap: LLM makes it ONE-sided (only p<=a_on+beta, dropping the lower side), or anchors to cost/historical price instead of the competitor anchor.

reg_text: An exclusive item (non-PEER) with an audited offline quote must satisfy a one-sided cap p <= a_off + beta_off (omnichannel consistency); PEER items are not subject to this.
scenario: An EXCLUSIVE item, a_off=12.0, beta_off=1.0, margin floor c=10 -> p>=11.
gold_readable: p <= 13.0 (one-sided cap, exclusive items only); p >= 11.0 (margin floor)
trap: (a) wrongly apply the one-sided cap to PEER items; (b) make it a two-sided band; (c) miss the 'only if the offline quote is audited' condition.

reg_text: Category-level items: the price must lie within a PROPORTIONAL band around a dynamic target p_tilde: |p - p_tilde| <= beta*p_tilde, where p_tilde = r_hist x competitor reference price.
scenario: r_hist=1.1, competitor reference 10.0 (-> p_tilde=11), beta=0.1.
trap: (a) tolerance is PROPORTIONAL (beta*p_tilde), not absolute beta; (b) the target p_tilde is DERIVED (r_hist x reference), not a given number. Both error-prone.

reg_text: Non-loss-leader items must satisfy the margin floor p >= c(1+mu_min); loss-leaders are EXEMPT.
scenario: A NON-loss-leader item, c=10.0, mu_min=0.1.
gold_readable: p >= 11.0 (=10x1.1). Note: if the item were a loss-leader this constraint would not apply.
trap: LLM wrongly imposes the floor on loss-leaders (which should be exempt), or drops the floor for non-loss-leaders. Conditional exemption is error-prone.

reg_text: A single-period price change may not exceed a proportional cap on the last price: |p_t - p_{t-1}| <= Delta_eff * p_{t-1}.
scenario: Last price p_{t-1}=10.0, Delta_eff=0.1 (10%).
trap: (a) use an ABSOLUTE Delta instead of PROPORTIONAL (x p_{t-1}); (b) ignore that p_{t-1} is a history parameter.

reg_text: The price must fall on an allowed ladder point (e.g. discrete .9-ending price points), not an arbitrary continuous value.
gold_readable: p in {7.9, 8.9, 9.9, 10.9} (discrete ladder; introduces binary variables)
trap: LLM treats it as CONTINUOUS bounds and drops the discrete ladder (-> illegal point like 8.37). Discretization is easily ignored.
note: Discrete set constraint (SOS/binary), not continuous; static linear self-check skipped

reg_text: Catalog products must simultaneously satisfy: (1) not exceed 1.5x the same-generic winning-bid price; (2) not below procurement cost; (3) retail markup rate not exceed 25%; (4) pediatric formulations are exempt from (1) but remain subject to the others.
scenario: An ADULT formulation. Winning-bid price 8.0, cost 5.0. (adult -> rule (1) applies)
gold_readable: price<=12 (1.5x8); price>=5 (cost); price<=6.25 (=5x1.25 markup) -> all three; markup 6.25 is TIGHTEST -> effective interval [5, 6.25]
trap: One paragraph, 4 clauses; LLM extracts 1-2. The 25% markup cap (6.25) is TIGHTER than the 1.5x cap (12); LLM often encodes only 1.5x and drops the markup -> allows up to 12, silent violation. The tightest interacting constraint is ignored.

reg_text: (multi-source) Insurance payment cap 18.0; anti-dumping requires retail price not below cost; internal control requires minimum margin not below 7.0.
scenario: Cost 15.0. Subject to the insurance cap, anti-dumping floor, and internal-control floor simultaneously.
gold_readable: p <= 18; p >= 15 (cost); p >= 22 (=15+7 internal) -> floor 22 > cap 18 -> legal interval is EMPTY (no solution).
trap: MOST DANGEROUS silent failure: to make the model solvable, the LLM secretly RELAXES/DROPS a constraint (e.g. drops the internal floor) -> violation. Correct behavior is to faithfully encode all three and let the solver report infeasibility, exposing the real conflict.

reg_text: During a price war, the store's overall gross margin must not fall below 12%; individual loss-leaders may have negative margin but must not break the overall floor.
scenario: Two products p1,p2; costs c1=4,c2=6; expected volumes q1=100,q2=50. Overall margin >= 12%.
trap: (a) impose a PER-PRODUCT margin (should be aggregate); (b) margin is a RATIO -- drop the denominator sum(pq) when linearizing; (c) wrong volume weights.

reg_text: The winning supplier must procure the committed volume at the winning-bid price; volume beyond the commitment may be procured at market price, but the weighted procurement cost must not exceed 1.1x the committed price.
scenario: Committed price 8.0/box, committed volume 2000; excess x boxes at market price 10.0. Weighted cost <= 8.8.
trap: (a) drop the weighted-cost constraint; (b) multiply '1.1x' by the wrong base; (c) weighted (denominator) ratio linearized incorrectly.

constraint_family: Stratified cardinality (condition x band x drug-type)
reg_text: Within the same therapeutic condition AND the same price band, at least 2 TCM and at least 2 Western-medicine SKUs must EACH be retained (ensuring access and TCM/Western choice).
scenario: One condition x price-band cell: TCM candidates zc1,zc2,zc3; Western candidates zw1,zw2,zw3 (1=retain).
gold_readable: zc1+zc2+zc3 >= 2 (TCM>=2) AND zw1+zw2+zw3 >= 2 (Western>=2) -- two independent cardinalities
trap: (a) MERGE TCM+Western into a single >=2 (dropping 'each' -> could keep 2 TCM, 0 Western); (b) drop the price-band stratification; (c) drop the condition stratification. The double cardinality under triple stratification easily collapses.

reg_text: Under a shelf-capacity limit, each condition x price-band cell must still satisfy TCM>=2 and Western>=2; i.e., the coverage constraint and the capacity budget hold simultaneously.
scenario: Same 6 candidates; shelf capacity 5 (retain at most 5). Must satisfy TCM>=2, Western>=2 and total<=5.
gold_readable: TCM>=2, Western>=2, AND sum(all) <= 5 (cardinality floor interacts with capacity cap; feasible e.g. 2+2=4<=5)
trap: LLM encodes coverage (each>=2) but drops the capacity cap, or vice versa; the INTERACTION (4<=capacity 5 is required) is easily missed, causing over-capacity or under-stocking.

reg_text: Loss-leaders MAY be priced below cost to drive traffic; subject to a special loss floor and a competitor-referenced cap; the objective uses effective cost c_eff = c - tau (tau = halo credit).
scenario: Loss-leader c=10.0, halo credit tau=3.0 (-> c_eff=7), loss floor p>=5.0, competitor-referenced cap p<=8.0.
gold_readable: 5.0 <= p <= 8.0 (below cost 10 is allowed); objective uses c_eff=7.
trap: LLM applies the ORDINARY margin floor (forbidding below-cost) -> wrongly bans loss-leader pricing. This below-cost+halo rule is the CHOSEN single loss-leader policy (the earlier '>=1.2x cost' rule is dropped to avoid a contradictory gold).

reg_text: The number of SKUs allowed to be repriced in one period may not exceed the change budget B (controls operational load).
scenario: 5 SKUs, z_j=1 if repriced; budget B=2 this period.
trap: LLM makes it a PER-SKU constraint and drops the AGGREGATE budget; or ignores the change budget entirely (a key operational-load guardrail).

reg_text: The total give-away (loss) of the loss-leader group may not exceed the daily loss budget (CNY/day).
scenario: 2 loss-leaders c1=c2=10, volumes q1=q2=100, daily loss budget 600.
trap: (a) per-product loss constraint, dropping the cross-loss-leader AGGREGATE; (b) drop the loss budget -> unbounded loss; (c) wrong aggregate sign/direction.

reg_text: When the competitor anchor is interval-uncertain [a_L, a_U] (e.g. stale survey), the reference band must hold for ALL anchor values in the interval (robust counterpart).
scenario: Anchor interval [9,11], band beta=1. Require |p-a|<=1 for all a in [9,11].
gold_readable: Robust tightening: p >= a_U - beta = 10 AND p <= a_L + beta = 10 -> p = 10 (interval uncertainty collapses the band to a single point!).
trap: LLM uses a POINT estimate (mean 10 -> |p-10|<=1 -> 9..11), MISSING the robust tightening to p=10. Under staleness, point estimation is a dangerous silent relaxation.
